rule_metadata.author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
rule_metadata.title: Suspicious Export Functionalities - Rundll32

rules.rule_text:
title: Suspicious Export Functionalities - Rundll32
id: 705d6f7b-779f-41c5-9994-8b191094577b
status: experimental
description: "Consider monitoring for instances of rundll32.exe running Windows \n\
  native DLLs that have export functionalities that adversaries commonly \nleverage\
  \ for executing malicious code and evading defensive controls. \nThe following pseudo-analytic\
  \ applies specifically to adversaries who \nuse the MiniDump export functionality\
  \ of comsvcs.dll to dump the \ncontents of LSASS, but this logic could be adapted\
  \ to detect other \nmalicious activity as well. Part of the RedCanary 2024 Threat\
  \ Detection \nReport.\n"
references:
- https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/techniques/rundll32/
author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
date: 2024/03/21
tags:
- attack.defense_evasion
- attack.t1218
- attack.t1218.011
- attack.credential_access
- attack.t1003
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection1:
    Image|endswith: \rundll32.exe
  selection2:
    CommandLine|contains: comsvcs.dll
  selection3:
    CommandLine|contains:
    - MiniDump
    - '#24'
  condition: all of selection*
falsepositives:
- Unknown
level: low
